anchor:
- **Position:** Senior Site Reliability Engineer – Observability Focus  
- **Seniority & Experience:** 5+ years as an SRE, with at least 3 years designing and operating large‑scale observability systems.  
- **Technical Skills Core:**  
  - Deep expertise in Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Jaeger or OpenTelemetry stack.  
  - Proven ability to build and maintain robust alerting rules, SLO/SLA dashboards, and incident response playbooks.  
  - Experience with distributed tracing, log aggregation (ELK, Fluentd, Splunk), and time‑series metrics.  
  - Advanced scripting in Python, Go or Rust; comfortable automating pipelines with Bash/Shell.  
  - Infrastructure‑as‑Code proficiency (Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation).  
- **Cloud & Orchestration:**  
  - Hands‑on with AWS (EKS, CloudWatch), GCP (GKE, Stackdriver) or Azure (AKS, Log Analytics).  
  - Kubernetes cluster ops, Helm, and service mesh (Istio, Linkerd) knowledge a plus.  
- **CI/CD & Deployment:**  
  - CI tools such as GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI, or Jenkins; solid understanding of Blue/Green, Canary deployments.  
  - Familiarity with automated rollback and canary analysis metrics.  
- **Soft Skills:**  
  - Strong incident response leadership – runbook ownership, root‑cause analysis, post‑mortem documentation.  
  - Excellent communication across cross‑functional teams (dev, product, security).  
- **Certifications:**  
  - AWS Certified SysOps Administrator or equivalent.  
  - Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) preferred.  
- **Other Requirements:**  
  - 24/7 on‑call rotation experience in a highly available SaaS environment.  
  - Proven ability to scale observability tools from <100 to >10k services with minimal latency impact.  
  - Demonstrated work in a rapidly growing startup (2–10k ARR).  
- **Location & Remote Policy:**  
  - Based in the U.S. with flexible fully‑remote options (remote preferred, but local to major tech hubs favored).  
- **Employment Type:** Full‑time, permanent; open to contract-to-hire if aligning.  
- **Start Date:** ASAP (preferred start within 4–6 weeks).  
- **Security Clearance:** None required.  

Looking for a candidate who can jump in immediately, build out the Observable Operations platform, and set standards for SLO-driven resilience.

positive.summary: Senior Site Reliability Engineer with 6 years of experience designing and operating large‑scale observability systems for SaaS platforms, scaling from 100 to 10k+ services with minimal latency.

work_experience.description: Automated Kubernetes cluster ops using Helm, deployed microservices on AWS EKS; set up CloudWatch monitoring and CloudFormation templates; performed incident analysis and post‑mortem documentation.

Built and operated large‑scale observability stack; implemented Fluentd + ELK for log aggregation, managed Prometheus metrics, automated canary & blue/green deployments via GitHub Actions & Jenkins; authored SLO driven dashboards and incident playbooks.

Lead architect of the Observability Ops Platform, scaling Prometheus federation and Grafana dashboards for 12k+ microservices; designed incident response playbooks and SLO dashboards; managed 24/7 on‑call rotation.

projects.name: Observability Ops Platform
projects.description: Platform delivering unified metrics, logs, and traces for 12k+ services; integrated Prometheus federation, Grafana, Loki, OpenTelemetry, and Jaeger.

projects.name: Canary Deployment Automation
projects.description: CI/CD pipeline for canary releases with automated rollback based on SLO metrics and alert data.

projects.name: Incident Response Playbook Hub
projects.description: Repository of runbooks, root‑cause analysis templates, and post‑mortem wiki linked to PagerDuty.

Developed reusable UI components in React/Next.js and maintained a strict component library. Utilized Tailwind CSS, Storybook, and Cypress for E2E testing. Configured CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions and Docker. Integrated Auth0 for user management and secured API interactions.

Lead the development of a CSS‑first component library with 300+ reusable components. Integrated Stripe Checkout elements, Auth0 authentication, and next‑image pipeline for WebP/AVIF. Implemented server‑side data fetching with SWR and TanStack Query, enforced API contracts via Postman/Swagger/OpenAPI. Maintained CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Vercel, Netlify, and Docker containerization. Achieved Core Web Vitals <1s across all projects.

anchor:
Senior Machine Learning Engineer – PyTorch & LLMs (Full‑Time, On‑Site HQ or Remote)  
‑ 5+ years commercial experience building and deploying large language models in production (e.g., LLaMA, GPT‑NeoX, or Google PaLM) using PyTorch 1.12+ and the latest Hugging Face Transformers (>=4.35).  
‑ Deep expertise in fine‑tuning and optimizing transformer architectures, quantization, and distributed training (NCCL, Horovod, or PyTorch Distributed Data Parallel).  
‑ Strong background in data processing pipelines with Pandas ≥ 1.5 and Dask, and experience scaling batch and streaming analytics on Kubernetes or AWS EMR.  
‑ Proficiency in writing clean, modular Python 3.10+ code, auto‑ML tooling (MLflow, DVC), and CI/CD integration in GitLab CI or GitHub Actions.  
‑ Experience with GPU‑accelerated inference (NVIDIA A100/ A6000) and model serving (TorchServe, Triton Inference Server).  
‑ Demonstrated ability to tune model hyperparameters (learning rates, batch sizes, prompt engineering) for maximum throughput and minimal latency.  
‑ Hands‑on with data privacy frameworks (GDPR, HIPAA) and secure data handling – experience of managing data encryption at rest and in transit is a plus.  
‑ Strong communication skills to translate business requirements into model specifications and to document model decisions in OWASP’s Maturity Model.  
‑ Preferred: PhD in ML/NLP or equivalent, open‑source contributions, or relevant industry certifications (AWS ML or GCP AI).  
‑ Ready to start within 4–6 weeks.  Apply via LinkedIn or our recruiting portal.

positive.summary: Senior Machine Learning Engineer with over 7 years of experience building, fine‑tuning, and deploying state‑of‑the‑art large language models in production across AWS and Kubernetes environments.

work_experience.description: Built data processing pipelines with Pandas, Dask, and AWS EMR for large text corpora. Developed and optimized transformer models using HuggingFace and PyTorch, applying techniques such as quantization and prompt engineering. Ensured compliance with GDPR and HIPAA by encrypting datasets at rest and in transit, and implementing secure data handling practices.

Lead the development and deployment of large language models for conversational AI, including fine‑tuning LLaMA and GPT‑NeoX. Designed distributed training pipelines using PyTorch DDP and Horovod on Kubernetes clusters, and built end‑to‑end MLOps workflows with MLflow, DVC, and GitLab CI. Implemented GPU‑accelerated inference on NVIDIA A100, serving models via TorchServe and Triton Inference Server.

projects.description: Automated pipeline for fine‑tuning the LLaMA model on domain‑specific corpora with distributed training and hyperparameter search.

projects.description: Engine for scaling GPT‑NeoX training across multi‑GPU clusters using PyTorch DDP and Horovod, achieving linear speedup.

projects.name: MLOps Pipeline for LLM Deployment

Senior Frontend Engineer (React 18.x, Next.js 14.x, Tailwind CSS v3.3)  
- 5+ years of production‑grade frontend development experience, with a strong focus on high‑performance SPA and multi‑page applications.  
- Proven expertise building component libraries with React Hooks, TSX + TypeScript (strict mode) and React Server Components, as well as edge‑function deployment on Vercel or Cloudflare Workers.  
- Hands‑on experience with GraphQL (Apollo Client) and REST APIs, including Apollo Server data caching, pagination and seamless client‑side state sync (RTK Query or React Query).  
- Comfortable with static‑site generation (getStaticProps) and incremental static regeneration in Next.js; mastery of custom _app.js, _document.js, and preview mode workflows.  
- Tailwind CSS architecture: custom plugin development, JIT mode, CSS purging, and utility‑first design system integration; at least 2 years of building responsive, accessible, high‑fidelity UI components using Tailwind.  
- Test‑first culture: Jest ≥27 + React Testing Library for unit & integration tests; end‑to‑end Cypress with partial mock of backend services; mandatory test coverage reporting in CI.  
- Build tooling: Vite 5.x (or Next.js 13+ built‑in), ESLint 8.x, Prettier 3.x, Husky + Lint‑staged, and npm / pnpm workflows. Familiar with bit.dev or component-driven DevOps pipelines.  
- Deployment & CI/CD: GitHub Actions with workflow caching, automatic code‑review assessments, and observability via Sentry or LogRocket.  
- Mentorship: Prior experience coaching junior developers, conducting code reviews, and driving best‑practice adoption (Agile, SCRUM, or Kanban).  
- Preferred: 3+ years in enterprise SaaS or financial tech environments; experience with GDPR‑compliant front‑ends; early‑access to upcoming React and Tailwind releases.  
- Employment: Full‑time, no C2C; based in Berlin but flexible remote (2–3 days in office); start date available ASAP, 30‑day notice.  
- Security: Clean background check; basic (Level II) security clearance required.

anchor:
Senior Principal Cybersecurity Engineer – Penetration Testing & IAM (Hybrid – New York, NY | Remote Eligible, 100% FT)

Seeking an accomplished Cybersecurity Architect with 10+ years of demonstrable expertise in both Offensive Security (Penetration Testing, Red Team Operations) and Identity & Access Management Frameworks (IAM, SSO, Privileged Access Management). The ideal candidate will possess deep proficiency in OWASP Top 10 exploitation, Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) simulation, and secure identity design in large-scale SaaS/On‑Prem environments. Must hold industry‑recognized certifications including OSCP, CEH, CISSP, and preferably SSCP or CISA. Experience developing and leading complex, high‑visibility engagements for Fortune 500 clients, and designing IAM solutions that align with NIST Cybersecurity Framework and ISO 27001 controls, is essential. The role requires a proactive, data‑driven mindset, excellent cross‑functional collaboration skills, and the ability to mentor mid‑level engineers.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead penetration testing teams on enterprise‑grade assessments ensuring comprehensive coverage of application, network, and cloud architectures.
- Architect IAM solutions that streamline authentication, authorization, and audit logging across a hybrid IT ecosystem.
- Conduct threat modeling and vulnerability remediation workshops with C‑level stakeholders.
- Drive continuous improvement of security posture metrics and KPIs.

Qualifications:
- 10+ years in cybersecurity roles with documented success in penetration testing and IAM initiatives.
- Advanced penetration testing skills (Metasploit, Burp Suite, custom exploit development) and experience with automated tools (Nessus, Qualys, Tenable).
- Mastery of IAM protocols (SAML, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect) and experience with leading IAM platforms (Okta, Azure AD, SailPoint, Ping Identity).
- Strong project management background and proven ability to deliver large-scale security solutions on time and within budget.
- Excellent communication and stakeholder management in a matrixed corporate environment.

Employment Details:
- Full‑time, contract-to-hire, 100% remote flexibility with occasional travel to New York HQ.
- Immediate availability (or within 30‑days) required.
- US security clearance (Secret) preferred, but not mandatory for initial engagements.

If you meet these qualifications and are interested in shaping the next generation of enterprise security, please submit your resume and a résumé of relevant projects to our recruiting portal.

Experience developing and leading complex, high‑visibility engagements for Fortune 500 clients, and designing IAM solutions that align with NIST Cybersecurity Framework and ISO 27001 controls, is essential. The role requires a proactive, data‑driven mindset, excellent cross‑functional collaboration skills, and the ability to mentor mid‑level engineers.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead penetration testing teams on enterprise‑grade assessments ensuring comprehensive coverage of application, network, and cloud architectures.
- Architect IAM solutions that streamline authentication, authorization, and audit logging across a hybrid IT ecosystem.
- Conduct threat modeling and vulnerability remediation workshops with C‑level stakeholders.
- Drive continuous improvement of security posture metrics and KPIs.

Qualifications:
- 10+ years in cybersecurity roles with documented success in penetration testing and IAM initiatives.
- Advanced penetration testing skills (Metasploit, Burp Suite, custom exploit development) and experience with automated tools (Nessus, Qualys, Tenable).
- Mastery of IAM protocols (SAML, OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect) and experience with leading IAM platforms (Okta, Azure AD, SailPoint, Ping Identity).
- Strong project management background and proven ability to deliver large-scale security solutions on time and within budget.
- Excellent communication and stakeholder management in a matrixed corporate environment.

Employment Details:
- Full‑time, contract-to-hire, 100% remote flexibility with occasional travel to New York HQ.
- Immediate availability (or within 30‑days) required.
- US security clearance (Secret) preferred, but not mandatory for initial engagements.

If you meet these qualifications and are interested in shaping the next generation of enterprise security, please submit your resume and a résumé of relevant projects to our recruiting portal.

Seeking a **Senior Full‑Stack Engineer (MERN + GraphQL)** to join our rapidly scaling fintech startup.  
**Key requirements:**  
- **React 18+** (hooks, context API, suspense), with redux toolkit or Zustand.

**Compliance & certifications:** Mandatory SOC 2 Type II (data center) clearance; HIPAA‑compliant handling of patient data. Prefer candidates with a **CISSP** or **AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional**.

Senior Principal Data Scientist – PyTorch & LLM Lead  
Full‑Time, Hybrid (New York/Los Angeles) | Remote Eligible – $200 k – 250 k Base + Sign‑On + Equity  
10+ Years of Experience | Ph.D. / M.S. in Computer Science, Statistics, or related discipline  

**Core Responsibilities**  
- Architect, develop, and deploy end‑to‑end deep learning solutions using PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, and large language models.  
- Own the full ML/AI lifecycle: data engineering, feature engineering (Pandas, Spark), model training, hyper‑parameter optimization, model interpretation, production monitoring, and data‑driven experimentation.  
- Lead a 6–8‑person data science squad, setting technical direction, setting performance benchmarks, and championing best practices in MLOps, reproducibility, and model governance.  
- Partner with product, platform, and business stakeholders to translate strategic objectives into actionable AI use‑cases with measurable ROI.  

**Required Expertise**  
- Proven depth in PyTorch, CUDA, distributed training, and model scaling techniques.  
- Extensive experience with transformers & large‑scale language models (GPT‑3/4, LLaMA, etc.), including fine‑tuning, alignment, and safe‑deployment practices.  
- Advanced proficiency in Pandas, NumPy, SciPy, and data‑pipeline orchestration.  
- Mastery of end‑to‑end machine‑learning operations (MLFlow, SageMaker, KFServing).  
- Demonstrated track record of delivering production‑grade models in regulated (FINRA, HIPAA, PCI‑DSS) environments.  
- Strong quantitative skills: statistical inference, causal modeling, Bayesian methods, and experiment design.  

**Leadership & Strategic Qualities**  
- Senior‑level communicator, capable of translating complex technical concepts to executive‑level audiences.  
- Proven mentorship of junior scientists and engineers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and innovation.  
- Experience steering multiple concurrent projects, balancing speed to market with rigorous validation.  

**Preferred Credentials**  
- Active or previously held security clearance (TS/SCI, U.S. government).  
- Familiarity with EU/UK GDPR‑compliant data practices.  
- Published peer‑reviewed papers or patents in state‑of‑the‑art deep‑learning.  

**Compensation & Benefits**  
- Competitive salary, quarterly performance bonuses, and equity for FYI.  
- Comprehensive health, dental, vision, and wellness packages.  
- 4‑week paid parental leave, flexible remote work policy, and generous PTO.  

**Next Steps**  
If you are a seasoned AI leader with a passion for building world‑class language‑modeling systems and driving enterprise impact, please submit your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your most successful model‑deployment case studies.  Start date preferred ASAP – we are agile, and we expect rapid onboarding.

- Experience with DAO governance frameworks (Aragon, Snapshot, Gnosis Safe) and on‑chain voting systems.

anchor:
Senior Full‑Stack Engineer – MERN + GraphQL, Technical Lead (Hybrid – New York City / Remote Options) – Full‑Time, No C2C

We are recruiting for a seasoned Full‑Stack Engineer to join our high‑velocity product engineering team, leading a squad that designs, implements, and maintains scalable web applications in a rapid‑release environment.  

Key Qualifications  
• 8 + years of professional experience designing and delivering production‑grade MERN (MongoDB, Express.js, React, Node.js) stack applications with a strong emphasis on GraphQL (Apollo Server, Apollo Client) and RESTful services.  
• Proven track record as a Technical Lead or Principal Engineer, responsible for architectural decisions, code quality standards, and mentoring mid‑level developers.  
• Deep expertise in TypeScript, ESNext, CSS‑in‑JS, and modern state‑management patterns (Redux, Zustand, context API).  
• Extensive experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI), automated testing (Jest, Cypress, Playwright), and container orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes).  
• Hands‑on experience deploying to multi‑region cloud environments (AWS, Azure, or GCP), including infrastructure‑as‑code (Terraform/CloudFormation) and managed services (RDS, DynamoDB, CloudFront).  
• Strong knowledge of security best practices – OWASP Top 10, JWT/OAuth, and experience implementing enterprise‑grade authentication (Okta, Auth0).  
• Demonstrated ability to drive cross‑functional collaboration with product, design, and QA, delivering solutions that meet strict SLAs and performance benchmarks.  

Desired Profile  
• Prior exposure to startup or fast‑growth tech environments (3+ years).  
• AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate (or comparable) is highly desirable.  
• Excellent communication skills, fluent in English, and ability to articulate complex technical concepts to non‑technical stakeholders.  
• Availability to start within 4–6 weeks; relocation assistance available for New York City candidates.  

If you possess these qualifications and thrive in a dynamic, high‑impact engineering culture, please share your updated resume and portfolio of relevant projects.

anchor:
Seeking a Senior Penetration Tester/Security Operations Consultant with 6‑10 years of hands‑on experience in enterprise‑grade red‑team engagements and SOC incident response. Must be fluent in **Kali Linux 2023.x**, **Metasploit Framework 6.0+**, **Burp Suite Professional 2024**, **OWASP ZAP 2.12+**, and scripting in **Python 3.10+** for custom exploitation tools. Experience required with **PowerShell Empire 2.0** for post‑exploitation on Windows, **Impacket 0.9.20** for SMB/NetBIOS enumeration, and **Nmap 7.95** for advanced scanning.  

SOC background: 4+ years working with **Splunk Enterprise 8.2+** and **Splunk Enterprise Security (ES) 5.9+**, including custom **Base64/CBCT** correlation rules, **SIEM** ingestion pipelines via **Beats 7.16** and **Logstash 7.16**, and root‑cause analysis with **MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise Navigator 1.6**.  
Should possess hands‑on experience with **SentinelOne** and **CrowdStrike Falcon** for EDR correlation, and familiarity with **Elastic Stack 7.16** for threat hunting.

IAM expertise: at least 3 years managing **Okta Identity Cloud** (enrollment, SAML 2.0/OIDC, lifecycle automation), **Azure AD Identity Protection**, and **Ping Identity** integration. Must be comfortable designing MFA policies, producing access review reports, and automating password rotation with **HashiCorp Vault 1.12**.

Certifications: **OSCP** and **CISSP** (current) preferred; **CCSP** or **GCIH** a plus. Security clearance at **Top‑Secret** or **SCI** required.  

Location: Seattle, WA (Hybrid – 3 days on‑site, 2 remote). Full‑time, no C2C, contract to hire possible. Must be available to start within 4 weeks.

anchor:
**Recruiter Search Query – Senior Platform & Infrastructure Lead (Kafka / Spark / dbt)**  

We are actively sourcing a **Senior Platform & Infrastructure Engineer** (Director/Head‑Level) to architect, implement, and govern end‑to‑end data pipelines on our cloud platform. Candidates must have proven experience leading multi‑disciplinary teams in designing distributed streaming and batch data solutions using **Apache Kafka, Apache Spark, and dbt**.  
**Core Competencies:**  
- 8+ years of progressive experience in building large‑scale data infrastructure (akin to a platform engineering or data engineering leader).  
- Deep expertise in stream processing (Kafka, Pulsar, or equivalent) and batch frameworks (Spark, Flink, Dataflow).  
- Advanced proficiency with modern ELT tools, specifically **dbt** for data transformation and modeling.  
- Robust knowledge of cloud data platforms (AWS Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks).  
- Hands‑on experience with IaC (Terraform, CloudFormation), CI/CD pipelines, and Kubernetes orchestration (EKS, GKE, AKS).  
- Strong background in performance tuning, scalability, and high‑availability design for data pipelines (RTS/ETL).  
- Demonstrated ability to architect and enforce data governance, security, and best‑practice standards.  
**Desired Background & Soft Skills:**  
- Prior leadership role (Team Lead, Lead Engineer, or Director) overseeing 3+ platform engineers and cross‑functional stakeholders.  
- Track record of driving large‑scale projects from concept through production deployment, with measurable KPIs around throughput, latency, and cost.  
- Comfortable deploying solutions in hybrid/multi‑cloud environments and navigating vendor ecosystems (AWS, GCP, Azure).  
- Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts to senior executives.  
**Employment & Location:**  
- Full‑time, exempt, permanent position (no C2C engagements).  
- Headquartered in Palo Alto, CA (with proximity to our satellite offices).  Remote candidates are considered for an in‑person “remote‑so‑you‑come‑in” arrangement at least 3 days per week.  
- Expected start date: Q3 2026, with flexible transition for the top candidate.  
**Security & Certifications (Optional):**  
- Security clearance (Top‑Secret) is a plus but not mandatory.  
- Certifications such as AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional, BigQuery Data Engineer, or Databricks Certified Data Engineer should be highlighted.  

If you know a seasoned platform engineer with the above skill set and leadership experience, please share their profile or contact me directly for a confidential discussion.

anchor:
**Recruiter Search Query – Senior Platform & Infrastructure Lead (Kafka / Spark / dbt)**  

We are actively sourcing a **Senior Platform & Infrastructure Engineer** (Director/Head‑Level) to architect, implement, and govern end‑to‑end data pipelines on our cloud platform. Candidates must have proven experience leading multi‑disciplinary teams in designing distributed streaming and batch data solutions using **Apache Kafka, Apache Spark, and dbt**.  
**Core Competencies:**  
- 8+ years of progressive experience in building large‑scale data infrastructure (akin to a platform engineering or data engineering leader).  
- Deep expertise in stream processing (Kafka, Pulsar, or equivalent) and batch frameworks (Spark, Flink, Dataflow).  
- Advanced proficiency with modern ELT tools, specifically **dbt** for data transformation and modeling.  
- Robust knowledge of cloud data platforms (AWS Redshift, BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks).  
- Hands‑on experience with IaC (Terraform, CloudFormation), CI/CD pipelines, and Kubernetes orchestration (EKS, GKE, AKS).  
- Strong background in performance tuning, scalability, and high‑availability design for data pipelines (RTS/ETL).  
- Demonstrated ability to architect and enforce data governance, security, and best‑practice standards.  
**Desired Background & Soft Skills:**  
- Prior leadership role (Team Lead, Lead Engineer, or Director) overseeing 3+ platform engineers and cross‑functional stakeholders.  
- Track record of driving large‑scale projects from concept through production deployment, with measurable KPIs around throughput, latency, and cost.  
- Comfortable deploying solutions in hybrid/multi‑cloud environments and navigating vendor ecosystems (AWS, GCP, Azure).  
- Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts to senior executives.  
**Employment & Location:**  
- Full‑time, exempt, permanent position (no C2C engagements).  
- Headquartered in Palo Alto, CA (with proximity to our satellite offices).  Remote candidates are considered for an in‑person “remote‑so‑you‑come‑in” arrangement at least 3 days per week.  
- Expected start date: Q3 2026, with flexible transition for the top candidate.  
**Security & Certifications (Optional):**  
- Security clearance (Top‑Secret) is a plus but not mandatory.  
- Certifications such as AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional, BigQuery Data Engineer, or Databricks Certified Data Engineer should be highlighted.  

If you know a seasoned platform engineer with the above skill set and leadership experience, please share their profile or contact me directly for a confidential discussion.

work_experience.description: Developed real-time data ingestion services using Apache Kafka and Spark Streaming, feeding transactional data into downstream analytics platforms. Built monitoring dashboards in Grafana and Prometheus, and automated deployment pipelines via Jenkins.

Designed and maintained batch ETL pipelines using Apache Spark and dbt, loading data into Amazon Redshift and Google BigQuery. Implemented infrastructure as code with Terraform and CloudFormation, ensuring reproducibility across AWS and GCP environments. Collaborated with data science and analytics teams to deliver high-quality, governed data assets.

Led the end-to-end design and implementation of data platforms serving 4 million daily transactions. Oversaw a team of 12 platform engineers and architected streaming pipelines with Apache Kafka, batch processing with Apache Spark, and transformation with dbt on Snowflake and Databricks. Deployed solutions using Terraform, CloudFormation, and Kubernetes on EKS and GKE. Drove a 30% reduction in data processing latency and 25% cost savings through performance tuning and resource optimization.

We are seeking a visionary, senior‑level Platform Engineer with 8+ years of hands‑on experience designing, deploying, and scaling high‑volume event‑driven data pipelines. The ideal candidate will possess:

* **Kafka**: Deep expertise in cluster design, partitioning strategy, ACLs, and performance optimization for millions of events per second.  
* **Apache Spark**: Robust knowledge of YARN/Dataproc/EKS cluster management, structured streaming, and Spark SQL optimization.  
* **dbt**: Proven track record building and maintaining modular data models, deployment automation, and orchestration via Airflow or Prefect.

* **Leadership & Mentoring**: Lead a team of 4–6 engineers, champion best practices, code reviews, and knowledge transfer. Drive architecture reviews and capacity planning.  
* **Security & Compliance**: GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA‑aware. Prior experience with TAM or network security role‑plays acceptable.  
* **Certifications**: Relevant certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Big Data – Specialty, Databricks Certified Professional) are a plus.  
* **Start Date**: Immediate availability.

anchor:
Seeking a Senior Platform & Infrastructure Engineer (Platform & Infrastructure – Kafka, Spark, dbt) to lead our data pipeline modernization effort. Candidate must have 7+ years of end‑to‑end data engineering experience, with proven expertise in:

- **Apache Kafka** 3.x cluster administration, including Confluent Platform 7.4, KRaft mode, and Kafka Connect 3.4 configuration for streaming integration to BigQuery and Snowflake.
- **Apache Spark** 3.4.x with Scala 2.12, Spark Structured Streaming 3.4, hive-exec 3.4, and optimized Delta Lake 1.8.1 on EMR or GCP Dataproc, including tuning of shuffle partitions, broadcast joins and resource allocation.
- **dbt** 1.6 with version control via dbt Core, dbt Cloud CI/CD pipelines, unit testing with dbt_utils, and integration with Snowflake DAG orchestration.
- **Cloud orchestration** (Kubernetes 1.27 or Terraform 1.7 for infra as code), CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions or Jenkins, and containerization with Docker 24.x.
- **Data security** compliance – experience with SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, and SSAE 18 audits, as well as AWS IAM or GCP IAM policies.
- **Experienced with** Kafka Streams, Flink, or Beam for data processing, and knowledge of Grafana/Prometheus for monitoring.

**Seniority & Experience:** 7–10 years in data engineering, with at least 3 years leading large-scale streaming pipelines and 2+ years designing and maintaining production-grade data platforms. Prior startup exposure is a plus.

**Location / Remote:** Remote‑first, based in the U.S. (Pacific or Mountain time preferred); occasional travel to headquarters (San Francisco, CA) weekly.

**Employment:** Full‑time, H1‑B vetted; we do not sponsor visas. Immediate start (within 4–6 weeks) for ready-to‑hire candidates. If you meet the above criteria and thrive in a fast‑paced, high‑impact team, please share your résumé and a brief LinkedIn summary.

positive.summary: Senior Platform & Infrastructure Engineer with 7.5 years of end-to-end data engineering experience, specializing in Kafka, Spark, dbt, and cloud orchestration across AWS and GCP. work_experience.description: Built real‑time fraud detection pipelines using Kafka 2.x, Kafka Streams, and Spark Structured Streaming. Deployed Delta Lake on EMR, secured data with AWS IAM policies, and stored artifacts on S3. Maintained data lake and lakehouse architecture, ensuring data security compliance (SOC 2). Implemented automated testing and monitoring with CloudWatch and Grafana.

Architected end-to-end streaming pipelines for order and logistics data, utilizing Kafka Streams, Flink, and Spark Structured Streaming. Implemented Delta Lake 1.8.1 on GCP Dataproc, optimized shuffle partitions and broadcast joins, achieving 30% faster query times. Developed dbt 1.6 transformations, automated CI/CD with Jenkins, and monitored pipelines with Prometheus and Grafana. Managed infrastructure as code with Terraform and Dockerized microservices for scalable deployment.

Lead the modernization of the data platform, managing a 3.x Kafka cluster with Confluent Platform 7.4 in KRaft mode, configuring Kafka Connect 3.4 for streaming ingestion to BigQuery and Snowflake. Designed and deployed Spark 3.4 on GCP Dataproc with Delta Lake 1.8.1, tuned structured streaming pipelines for low-latency analytics. Built dbt 1.6 pipelines in GitHub Actions, integrated with Snowflake DAG orchestration. Orchestrated infrastructure with Terraform 1.7 and Kubernetes 1.27, containerized services with Docker 24.x. Ensured SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, and SSAE 18 compliance through IAM policy hardening and audit-ready configurations.

anchor:
We are recruiting a Senior Backend Engineer with proven expertise in Go, Rust, Python, and Django frameworks to join our mission‑driven technology organization. The ideal candidate will possess a minimum of 8‑10 years of end‑to‑end backend development experience, a strong background in designing and deploying highly available, scalable micro‑services, and demonstrable leadership in architecting cloud‑native solutions. Applicants should demonstrate fluency in API design, distributed systems, CI/CD pipelines, and container orchestration (Kubernetes). Advanced proficiency in both relational (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL (Redis, Cassandra) data stores, coupled with deep knowledge of performance tuning, caching strategies, and data consistency models, is required. Candidates must hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field, possess excellent written and verbal communication skills, and exhibit strong collaboration across cross‑functional teams in an Agile environment. 

Preferred qualifications include: (1) prior experience leading a platform team with responsibility for mentorship and code‑review processes; (2) familiarity with AWS/Azure/GCP services and infrastructure‑as‑code (Terraform, Pulumi, CloudFormation); (3) experience with observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog) and distributed tracing; (4) certifications such as Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) or AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional; and (5) familiarity with security best practices, including OWASP Top Ten, TLS, and secure coding standards. A security clearance to access classified data or a proven track record with regulated sectors (government, defense, finance, healthcare) is a significant advantage. 

This is a full‑time, on‑site role located in the Washington, DC metropolitan region (Hybrid work model, 3 days onsite/2 days remote). The start date is flexible, subject to availability and successful reference checks. Candidates may provide their expected salary range; we offer competitive compensation, health benefits, equity, and additional perks aligned with our core values of innovation, integrity, and community impact. Apply today to drive transformative backend solutions and shape the future of our technology stack.

anchor:
Senior Embedded Systems Engineer (C/C++ & RTOS/FPGA) – 8+ yrs experience, AC/DC, automotive/industrial; Full‑time, onsite (Austin, TX) or hybrid (remote eligible within 1 hour of CST).   

**Core Skills (must‑have)**  
- C99/C++11 (+C++17) programming on ARM Cortex‑M4/M7, M33 or RISC‑V with GCC arm-none-eabi 10.2.1 or IAR 7.51  
- Real‑time OS: Deep experience with FreeRTOS 10.x (v10.3.1) or VxWorks 7 in statically‑linked kernel mode, including task priorities, queues, semaphores, and start‑up scripts (posix style)  
- FPGA design: Xilinx Vivado 2023.3 or Intel Quartus Prime 22.1, using Verilog‑2001 and SystemVerilog‑2012; verified through QuestaSim 10.0b or ModelSim SE 10.0; board‑level UART/PCIe debug and board bring‑up on Xilinx Zynq‑MP or Intel Arria‑V families  
- IDEs: Keil MDK‑ARM 5.25, IAR Embedded Workbench 7.51, or Visual Studio 2022 with PlatformIO  
- Version control & CI: Git (GitHub/GitLab), Jenkins CI pipelines (Windows/Linux), Docker builds for cross‑deployment  

**Additional Technical Requirements**  
- Automotive ECU experience: ISO 26262 ASIL‑B, Secure Boot, AUTOSAR Basic Software stack, and MISRA‑C:2012 compliance.  
- Embedded Linux: Yocto Project (Poky) 3.5 with Kconfig, Device Tree overlays for RPi‑4/Jetson‑Nano.  
- Communication Protocols: SPI, I2C, CAN‑FD, LIN, Ethernet (TCP/IP stack) and UDP.  
- Toolchain integration: Experience scripting with Python 3.11, Bash 5.1, and Jenkinsfile for build/test automation.  
- Security: Knowledge of secure boot/UEFI, hardware‑based root of trust (Trusted Platform Module v2), and static code analysis (Coverity, Cppcheck).  

**Certifications / Clearances**  
- RTOS certification (FreeRTOS+) or equivalent; Xilinx FPGA Design certification preferred.  
- SF‑10 clearance or higher for defense or aerospace projects.  

**Preferences**  
- 6+ years in automotive or aerospace embedded domain; startup exposure is a plus.  
- Fluent in English, comfort with Japanese or German (project‑specific).  

**Start Date & Relocation**  
- Immediate start; relocation stipend if required.  

Please send CV + portfolio of RTOS/FPGA projects (public repos or demo videos) to hr@embedded‑hire.com.

work_experience.description: Implemented automotive ECUs for AC/DC conversion modules using ARM Cortex-M4, IAR EW 7.51, and VxWorks 7. Developed comprehensive test suites with Jenkins pipelines and Docker containers.

Designed and validated CAN-FD transceiver cores on Xilinx Zynq‑MP using Vivado 2023.3, Verilog‑2001, SystemVerilog-2012, and tested with QuestaSim 10.0b. Integrated UART and PCIe debug for board bring‑up.
Spearheaded the development of AC/DC power management SoCs for automotive ECUs using ARM Cortex-M7, FreeRTOS 10.3.1, and secure boot. Led ISO 26262 ASIL-B certification and MISRA-C:2012 compliance.

anchor: Seeking an experienced Senior Solidity Engineer (5+ years) to join our on‑prem flagship blockchain platform team. The role is full‑time, hybrid (NYC‑HQ, remote allowed for candidates with a U.S. security clearance). Must be proficient with Solidity 0.8.x, execution of audited ERC‑20, ERC‑721, and ERC‑4626 token contracts, and have hands‑on experience deploying to Ethereum mainnet (via Hardhat v2.12 or Foundry v0.4) and Layer‑2 solutions (Optimism, Arbitrum, Polygon). Desired expertise includes use of OpenZeppelin Contracts v5.0, upgradeable proxy patterns (Transparent and UUPS), and integration with Foundry test suites or Brownie. Candidates must demonstrate prior work on a multi‑signature wallet or DAO module, with demonstrable audit logs (Consensys Diligence or CertiK). Cryptographic knowledge (SM2, ECDSA, BLS12‑381) and familiarity with zk‑SNARK/zk‑STARK tooling (SnarkJS, ZoKrates) is highly valued. Strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions) that automatically runs test coverage, gas cost analysis, and deployment scripts. Please specify experience with contract upgradability via OpenZeppelin Upgrades plugin and ability to remediate reentrancy and front‑running vulnerabilities in a real world scenario (e.g., using Provable or Keep3r). anchor:
Seeking a Senior Full‑Stack Engineer (MERN + GraphQL) to join our high‑growth, enterprise‑grade product engineering team. The role is based in our New York City headquarters with hybrid onsite/remote availability, and requires **8+ years of full‑stack development experience**. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Mastery of **React, React‑Redux, Node.js, Express, MongoDB** and **Apollo GraphQL**; architecture of complex, mission‑critical services.  
- Proven experience leading cross‑functional teams, defining coding standards, guiding junior engineers, and managing release pipelines in an Agile environment.  
- Fluency in **RESTful API design, GraphQL schema design, authentication/authorization (OAuth2, JWT), CI/CD (Jenkins/Travis), Docker, Kubernetes** and optionally **AWS (RDS, S3, Lambda)**.  
- Ability to translate product requirements into scalable, secure, and maintainable solutions; coaching others on best design patterns.  
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering or related field (MS preferred).  
- Professional certifications (AWS Solutions Architect, Microsoft, or equivalent) are a plus.  

We are a full‑time, direct‑hire transition with no C2C or contractor arrangements. The compensation package is commensurate with experience and includes comprehensive benefits, equity, and a relocation stipend.  
**Start date:** ASAP/within 4 weeks of offer.  

If you are a data‑driven, people‑first leader with a passion for cutting‑edge web technologies and a track record of delivering high‑availability products, we invite you to apply.

anchor:
Senior Mobile Engineer (iOS & Android, Swift 5.7 / Kotlin 1.9) – 5+ years + remote, full‑time (C2C acceptable)

We’re building a cross‑platform health app and need a senior mobile engineer who can ship production‑grade code on both iOS and Android. The role requires:

**iOS**  
- Swift 5.7+ (Xcode 15.x) – must have deep experience building inter‑app extensions, WidgetKit, App Clip, and SwiftUI 4+, including Swift Package Manager and Binary curation.  
- Core Data + Realm ODB mastery, protocol‑centric architecture, Combine + async/await pipelines.  
- CI/CD pipeline built on Fastlane + GitHub Actions, and integration with Apple TestFlight + App Store Connect.  
- Unit testing with XCTest, UI testing with XCUITest, automated UI tests on device farms (AWS Device Farm or Firebase Test Lab).  
- Experience with App Transport Security, keychain encryption, and Apple’s recent privacy impact assessments.

**Android**  
- Kotlin 1.9 (Android Studio 4.2+), Jetpack Compose 1.5+ for UI, modern architecture components (ViewModel, StateFlow, LiveData).  
- Dagger 2 / Hilt + Retrofit 2.x for DI & networking, Coroutines + Flow for async.  
- Android Jetpack Navigation, WorkManager, Room persistence with test‑driven data models.  
- CI/CD via GitLab CI or CircleCI, Android Release with Fastlane + Play Store API.  
- UI testing with Espresso, UI Automator, integration tests with Robolectric or instrumented tests on Firebase Test Lab.

**Other Requirements**  
- 5+ years of professional mobile development, 3+ years leading a small team or mentoring.  
- Sound knowledge of continuous delivery, OTA updates, and streaming analytics.  
- Deep understanding of JNI/NDK for cross‑platform libraries (C++/Rust) is a plus.  
- Experience shipping to US healthcare (HIPAA) or fintech (PCI‑DSS) is highly desirable.  
- Ability to write clean, well‑tested, maintainable code; comfortable with Git branching strategies and code reviews.  
- Must be eligible for a secret or top‑secret security clearance (if this is a DoD or high‑stakes contract).  

Preferred extras:  
- Apple Certified iOS Developer (ACID), Google Certified Android Engineer, or equivalent dev‑ops certifications (AWS/Azure).  
- Native iOS UI/UX awards or notable open‑source contributions for bonus points.

Location: Remote (US‑based recommended). Start date: ASAP, 2‑week notice acceptable. Salary: Top of market for senior mobile engineers, bonus & equity options. Apply with a one‑page résumé, portfolio links, and a 600‑word narrative of a recent performance‑critical feature you built.

Senior Penetration Tester – Full‑Time (Remote)  
*Location: US (Moscow, LA, or New York) – must be eligible for SOC-3 background check; Top Secret clearance required*  

**Key Requirements**  
- 5+ years of hands‑on Red Team / Pen Test experience  
- Deep expertise with Kali Linux (2024.x) and Kali tools (nmap 7.93, sqlmap 1.4.12, recon-ng 0.5.5)  
- Proven mastery of Metasploit Pro 6.2 (including RapidLoad, WebApp modules) and Core Impact 3.5 (automation scripting)  
- Advanced Burp Suite 2023.3 (Intruder, Repeater, Extender) and OWASP ZAP 5.3 (Active Scanner, SuiteDash) proficiency  
- Hands‑on experience performing internal corporate network exploitation, privilege escalation, and post‑exploitation (Meterpreter)  
- Strong background in AWS IAM: policy authoring, role‑based access, identity federation, and AWS Security Hub integration  
- Okta/OneLogin SSO & MFA administration – configuration of SAML, OIDC, and custom policy rules  
- SOC operations: Splunk Enterprise 9.0 (indexing, alert orchestration), Azure Sentinel (Kusto queries, analytics), and ELK stack (Logstash 8.5, Kibana 8.5)  
- Languages: Fluent in English; Spanish fluency is a plus  

**Additional Preferences**  
- Prior experience with SASE/Zero Trust infrastructure, CloudGuard, Palo Alto PAN-OS 10.x  
- CISSP or OSCP certification preferred; CISSP‑ISM or GIAC‑MM in synergy with IAM/SOC best practices  
- Availability to onboard within 30–45 days; travel to HQ (Tokyo/Frankfurt) up to 20% of the year  

**Job Type**  
Full‑time, no C2C, direct hire. Remote work acceptable with a U.S. office in the stated regions. Salary commensurate with experience and certifications.

Senior Principal Embedded Systems Engineer – (C, C++, RTOS, FPGA) – 12+ Years Experience

We are seeking a senior‑level embedded systems engineer with a proven track record of architecting and developing high‑reliability, real‑time firmware for mission‑critical platforms. The ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in C/C++ programming, RTOS kernel internals, FPGA design (VHDL/Verilog), DMA engine configuration, and low‑level hardware interfacing. A minimum of 10–12 years of progressive experience in an automotive, aerospace or defense environment, with a demonstrable history of leading cross‑functional teams, is required.

Responsibilities will include defining architecture, driving design reviews, mentoring junior staff, and ensuring compliance with stringent quality and security standards. Active TOP SECRET or higher security clearance is mandatory, and the ability to obtain TS/SCI status is preferred. A professional certification such as IEEE Senior Member or Embark Systems Engineer is highly desirable.

Location: Austin, TX (hybrid remote – remote hours permissible within the U.S.). Employment: Full‑time, Direct Hire, no C2C or contract arrangements. Target start date: Immediate.
